qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.title: Denial of Service via missing ReadHeaderTimeout in HTTP server
qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.advisory_match_reason: Different vulnerable component and root cause: this finding targets Go net/http server header-read timeout omission in pkg/modules/api/api.go, not the advisory's Chromium file-scheme deny-list bypass caused by case-sensitive regexp matching in FilterDeadline.

qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.title: Server-Side Request Forgery via unfiltered download-from URL feature
qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.advisory_match_reason: Different FilterDeadline condition and caller path: this finding targets the downloadFrom feature's empty allow-list and deny-list defaults plus outbound HTTP fetching, while the advisory targets Chromium URL/HTML conversion where a mixed-case file: scheme bypasses the default case-sensitive chromium deny-list regex.

target_advisory_judgement.reason: No final qualifying_vuln finding identifies the advisory-described Chromium deny-list bypass root cause: the default chromium file: deny-list is matched case-sensitively in pkg/gotenberg/filter.go, allowing uppercase or mixed-case file schemes that Chromium later normalizes and uses to read local files.
evidence: Advisory GHSA-jjwv-57xh-xr6r describes a bypass of the default --chromium-deny-list regex ^file:(?!//\/tmp/).* because FilterDeadline compiles the deny-list with regexp flags 0 and therefore performs case-sensitive matching.

The advisory root cause involves pkg/modules/chromium/chromium.go default deny-list configuration, pkg/modules/chromium/events.go request filtering, and Chromium normalization of FILE: or mixed-case file schemes before local file navigation.

slowloris-read-header-timeout/vulnerability_report.json reports missing ReadHeaderTimeout in pkg/modules/api/api.go and a Slowloris resource-exhaustion attack path, with no Chromium URL filtering or file-scheme bypass.

ssrf-download-from/vulnerability_report.json reports downloadFrom accepting arbitrary URLs because API download allow-list and deny-list defaults are empty; it does not identify the non-empty chromium deny-list, case-sensitive file: scheme matching, Chromium request interception, or arbitrary local file read.
